label: National Rail – Normal Service – Single or Return

cancellation_windows.label: Early cancellation
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested 48 or more hours before scheduled departure

cancellation_windows.label: Standard cancellation
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested between 24 and 48 hours before scheduled departure

cancellation_windows.label: Late cancellation
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested between 2 and 24 hours before scheduled departure

cancellation_windows.label: No refund
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested less than 2 hours before departure, or after departure time has passed

return_ticket_notes: For return tickets, each leg is assessed independently. If the outbound leg has already been travelled, only the return leg amount is eligible for refund, assessed against the return departure time.
no_show_policy: Passengers who do not travel and do not cancel in advance are treated as a no-show. No refund is issued for no-shows.

label: National Rail – Express Service – Single or Return

cancellation_windows.label: Early cancellation
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested 48 or more hours before scheduled departure

cancellation_windows.label: Late cancellation
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested between 24 and 48 hours before scheduled departure

cancellation_windows.label: No refund
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested less than 24 hours before departure, or after departure time has passed

return_ticket_notes: Same as normal service: each leg assessed independently against its own departure time.
no_show_policy: No refund issued for no-shows on express services.

label: Metro – Single Ticket

cancellation_windows.label: Full refund
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested before the passenger has tapped in at the origin station
cancellation_windows.status_condition: Booking status is 'confirmed' and journey has not commenced

cancellation_windows.label: No refund
cancellation_windows.condition: Journey has already commenced (passenger has tapped in)
cancellation_windows.status_condition: Booking status is 'completed' or travelled_at is not null

notes: Metro single tickets are valid on the travel date only. A booking with status 'cancelled' is already cancelled and no further refund action is needed.

label: Metro – Day Pass

cancellation_windows.label: Full refund
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested before the passenger has made their first tap-in on the travel date
cancellation_windows.status_condition: Booking status is 'confirmed' and travelled_at is null

cancellation_windows.label: No refund
cancellation_windows.condition: Day pass has been activated (first tap-in recorded)
cancellation_windows.status_condition: Booking status is 'completed' or travelled_at is not null

notes: Day passes are non-transferable and valid only for the calendar day of purchase.

label: Delay Compensation – All Networks

compensation_rules.condition: Train delayed 30–59 minutes due to operator fault
compensation_rules.compensation: 50% refund of the fare for the delayed leg
compensation_rules.how_to_claim: Submit claim via app or customer service within 28 days of travel date

compensation_rules.condition: Train delayed 60–119 minutes due to operator fault
compensation_rules.compensation: 100% refund of the fare for the delayed leg
compensation_rules.how_to_claim: Submit claim via app or customer service within 28 days of travel date

compensation_rules.condition: Train delayed 120 minutes or more due to operator fault
compensation_rules.compensation: 100% refund of the fare for the delayed leg, plus reimbursement of alternative transport costs up to $10.00
compensation_rules.how_to_claim: Submit claim with receipts via customer service within 28 days of travel date

exclusions: Delays caused by third-party events (extreme weather, security incidents, government directives, or passenger-caused incidents) are not eligible for compensation.

label: Penalty Fares

applies_to.description: Applies to all passengers travelling on metro or national rail services without a valid ticket.

penalty_amount.rule: The penalty fare is the greater of $20.00 or five times the applicable single fare for the journey being made.

escalation.escalation_rule: If the initial penalty fare is unpaid within 14 days of issue, the amount escalates to $100.00 and may be referred to a debt collection agency.

when_penalty_applies: Presenting an expired, forged, or invalid ticket
Travelling beyond the destination shown on the ticket
Failing to present a ticket when asked by authorised staff
appeal_process: Passengers who believe a penalty fare was issued incorrectly may appeal within 14 days of the issue date via the customer service portal.

label: Engineering Works Compensation

entitlement.rule: If a national rail service is cancelled due to planned engineering works and the passenger was not notified of the cancellation at the time of booking, a full automatic refund is issued with no admin fee.
entitlement.trigger: Service cancelled due to planned engineering works; passenger not informed at booking

notification_standard: Passengers are considered 'notified' if the engineering works cancellation was published on the TransitFlow app, website, or by email at least 48 hours before the scheduled departure.
how_refund_is_processed: Refunds are processed automatically within 5 business days to the original payment method. No claim is required.
alternative_travel: Where engineering works affect a service, TransitFlow will endeavour to provide alternative travel arrangements (replacement bus service or alternative route). Passengers who decline the alternative and choose not to travel are still entitled to a full refund.
exclusions: This policy does not apply to delays caused by unplanned emergency engineering works or third-party infrastructure incidents.
